lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਰਾਮ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਕਹੈ ਕਹਿਐ ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: raam raam sabh ko kahai kahiai raam na hoe |
lines.english: Everyone chants the Lord's Name, Raam, Raam; but by such chanting, the Lord is not obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੂਜਰੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: goojaree mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Goojaree, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਕਾਰਣੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਕਰੇ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੪॥੩॥੫॥
lines.translit: eihu kaaran karataa kare jotee jot samaae |4|3|5|
lines.english: This deed was done by the Creator Lord; one's light merges into the Light. ||4||3||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਰਾਮੁ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਤਾ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਕੋਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee raam man vasai taa fal paavai koe |1|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, the Lord comes to dwell in the mind, and then, the fruits are obtained. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਗੋਵਿੰਦ ਜਿਸੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: antar govind jis laagai preet |
lines.english: One who enshrines love for God within his mind,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿਰਦੈ ਜਿਨੑ ਕੈ ਕਪਟੁ ਵਸੈ ਬਾਹਰਹੁ ਸੰਤ ਕਹਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: hiradai jina kai kapatt vasai baaharahu sant kahaeh |
lines.english: Those whose hearts are filled with hypocrisy, who are called saints only for their outward show

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਤਿਸੁ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਸਦਾ ਮਨਿ ਚੀਤਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har tis kade na veesarai har har kareh sadaa man cheet |1| rahaau |
lines.english: never forgets the Lord; he continually chants the Lord's Name, Har, Har, in his conscious mind. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਮੂਲਿ ਨ ਚੁਕਈ ਅੰਤਿ ਗਏ ਪਛੁਤਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: trisanaa mool na chukee ant ge pachhutaeh |2|
lines.english: - their desires are never satisfied, and they depart grieving in the end. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨੇਕ ਤੀਰਥ ਜੇ ਜਤਨ ਕਰੈ ਤਾ ਅੰਤਰ ਕੀ ਹਉਮੈ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: anek teerath je jatan karai taa antar kee haumai kade na jaae |
lines.english: Although one may bathe at many places of pilgrimage, still, his ego never departs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਨਰ ਕੀ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਧਰਮ ਰਾਇ ਤਿਸੁ ਦੇਇ ਸਜਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jis nar kee dubidhaa na jaae dharam raae tis dee sajaae |3|
lines.english: That man, whose sense of duality does not depart - the Righteous Judge of Dharma shall punish him. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਮਾਰੇ ਤਾਂ ਹਰਿ ਭੇਟੈ ਸੋਈ ॥੪॥੪॥੬॥
lines.translit: naanak vichahu haumai maare taan har bhettai soee |4|4|6|
lines.english: O Nanak, if one conquers his ego within, then he comes to meet the Lord. ||4||4||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੋਈ ਜਨੁ ਪਾਏ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: karam hovai soee jan paae guramukh boojhai koee |
lines.english: That humble being, unto whom God showers His Mercy, obtains Him; how few are the Gurmukhs who understand Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੂਜਰੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: goojaree mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Goojaree, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਜਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਹਰਿ ਚਰਣੀ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: so jan niramal ji guramukh boojhai har charanee chit laae |1|
lines.english: That humble being is immaculately pure, who, as Gurmukh, understands the Lord, and focuses his consciousness on the Lord's Feet. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਜਨ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਸਦਾ ਮਤਿ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਜਿਸ ਕਾ ਅਭਿਮਾਨੁ ਗਵਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: tis jan saant sadaa mat nihachal jis kaa abhimaan gavaae |
lines.english: That humble being who eliminates his ego is at peace; he is blessed with an ever-stable intellect.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਚੇਤਿ ਅਚੇਤ ਮਨਾ ਜੋ ਇਛਹਿ ਸੋ ਫਲੁ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: har chet achet manaa jo ichheh so fal hoee |
lines.english: O my unconscious mind, remain conscious of the Lord, and you shall obtain the fruits of your desires.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਪਾਵਹਿ ਪੀਵਤ ਰਹਹਿ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee har ras paaveh peevat raheh sadaa sukh hoee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, you shall obtain the sublime elixir of the Lord; by continually drinking it in, you shall have eternal peace. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਭੇਟੇ ਤਾ ਪਾਰਸੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਪਾਰਸੁ ਹੋਇ ਤ ਪੂਜ ਕਰਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: satigur bhette taa paaras hovai paaras hoe ta pooj karaae |
lines.english: When one meets the True Guru, he becomes the philosopher's stone, with the ability to transform others, inspiring them to worship the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਪਾਰਸੈ ਪੂਜ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਵਿਣੁ ਮਨ ਪਰਚੇ ਅਵਰਾ ਸਮਝਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: vin paarasai pooj na hovee vin man parache avaraa samajhaae |
lines.english: Without becoming the philosopher's stone, he does not inspire others to worship the Lord; without instructing his own mind, how can he instruct others?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਉਸੁ ਪੂਜੇ ਸੋ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਏ ਦੀਖਿਆ ਦੇਵੈ ਸਾਚੁ ਬੁਝਾਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jo us pooje so fal paae deekhiaa devai saach bujhaae |2|
lines.english: One who worships the Lord in adoration, obtains his rewards; instructing others, he reveals the Truth. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੂ ਸਦਾਏ ਅਗਿਆਨੀ ਅੰਧਾ ਕਿਸੁ ਓਹੁ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਪਾਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: guroo sadaae agiaanee andhaa kis ohu maarag paae |3|
lines.english: The ignorant, blind man calls himself the guru, but to whom can he show the way? ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਵਿਣੁ ਨਦਰੀ ਕਿਛੂ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ ਜਿਸੁ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਸੋ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: naanak vin nadaree kichhoo na paaeeai jis nadar kare so paae |
lines.english: O Nanak, without His Mercy, nothing can be obtained. One upon whom He casts His Glance of Grace, obtains Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਦੇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਅਪਣਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਵਰਤਾਏ ॥੪॥੫॥੭॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee de vaddiaaee apanaa sabad varataae |4|5|7|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, God bestows greatness, and projects the Word of His Shabad. ||4||5||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕਾਸੀ ਮਤਿ ਊਪਜੈ ਨਾ ਕਾਸੀ ਮਤਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naa kaasee mat aoopajai naa kaasee mat jaae |
lines.english: Wisdom is not produced in Benares, nor is wisdom lost in Benares.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੂਜਰੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ਪੰਚਪਦੇ ॥
lines.translit: goojaree mahalaa 3 panchapade |
lines.english: Goojaree, Third Mehl, Panch-Padhay:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਥਾ ਤੂੰ ਸੁਣਿ ਰੇ ਮਨ ਸਬਦੁ ਮੰਨਿ ਵਸਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har kathaa toon sun re man sabad man vasaae |
lines.english: Listen to the sermon of the Lord, O mind, and enshrine the Shabad of His Word within your mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਮਤਿ ਊਪਜੈ ਤਾ ਇਹ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai mat aoopajai taa ih sojhee paae |1|
lines.english: Meeting the True Guru, wisdom is produced, and then, one obtains this understanding. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਚਰਣ ਰਿਦੈ ਵਸਾਇ ਤੂ ਕਿਲਵਿਖ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਨਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: har charan ridai vasaae too kilavikh hoveh naas |
lines.english: Enshrine the Lord's lotus feet within your heart, and your sins shall be erased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹ ਮਤਿ ਤੇਰੀ ਥਿਰੁ ਰਹੈ ਤਾਂ ਭਰਮੁ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: eih mat teree thir rahai taan bharam vichahu jaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: If your intellect remains stable and steady, then doubt shall depart from within you. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਮੁਗਧੁ ਹੈ ਸੋਝੀ ਕਿਛੂ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh ihu man mugadh hai sojhee kichhoo na paae |
lines.english: This mind of the self-centered manmukh is so stupid; it does not obtain any understanding at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਭੂ ਆਤਮਾ ਵਸਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਤਾ ਤੀਰਥ ਕਰਹਿ ਨਿਵਾਸੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: panch bhoo aatamaa vas kareh taa teerath kareh nivaas |2|
lines.english: If your soul overcomes the five elements, then you shall come to have a home at the true place of pilgrimage. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਕਾਸੀ ਸਭਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਸਿਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੀਆ ਬੁਝਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man kaasee sabh teerath simrit satigur deea bujhaae |
lines.english: In this mind are found Benares, all sacred shrines of pilgrimage and the Shaastras; the True Guru has explained this.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਬੁਝਈ ਅੰਤਿ ਗਇਆ ਪਛੁਤਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har kaa naam na bujhee ant geaa pachhutaae |3|
lines.english: It does not understand the Name of the Lord; it departs repenting in the end. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਤਿਸੁ ਸੰਗਿ ਰਹਹਿ ਜਿਨ ਹਰਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਰਹਿਆ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: atthasatth teerath tis sang raheh jin har hiradai rahiaa samaae |4|
lines.english: The sixty-eight places of pilgrimage remain with one, whose heart is filled with the Lord. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਬੁਝਿਆ ਏਕੁ ਵਸਿਆ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur miliai hukam bujhiaa ek vasiaa man aae |
lines.english: O Nanak, upon meeting the True Guru, the Order of the Lord's Will is understood, and the One Lord comes to dwell in the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਭੁ ਸਚੁ ਹੈ ਸਚੇ ਰਹੈ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੫॥੬॥੮॥
lines.translit: jo tudh bhaavai sabh sach hai sache rahai samaae |5|6|8|
lines.english: Those who are pleasing to You, O True Lord, are true. They remain absorbed in You. ||5||6||8||
